mission.title: vitess: Bug Report: `SELECT *` expansion only considers subquery in column list on unsharded queries
mission.description: Bug Report: `SELECT *` expansion only considers subquery in column list on unsharded queries. This issue affects 5+ users.

steps.title: Identify vitess troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your vitess deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n vitess -l app.kubernetes.io/name=vitess
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=vitess -n vitess --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review vitess configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant vitess configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n vitess -l app.kubernetes.io/name=vitess
kubectl get configmap -n vitess -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=vitess
```
### Overview of the Issue

 encountered a bug where a subquery in the columns list of an inner query would be the only column expanded by the `SELECT *` expression of the outer query. This bug only exists in the Gen4 planner.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Bug Report: `SELECT *` expansion only considers subquery in
steps.description:
Before planning a query, vtgate will simplify the query. One of the simplifications is to expand `*` outputs in the query.
This is only done if we have enough schema information about the tables in the query.

The issue reported in #11880 is related to a derived table where we don't have enough information to expand the columns. Before this fix, we would just use whatever column information we had, which is not correct.

This PR changes the semantic analysis so it keeps track in more places if

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Confirm Bug Report: `SELECT *` expansion only considers is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=vitess -n vitess --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n vitess --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Before planning a query, vtgate will simplify the query. One of the simplifications is to expand `*` outputs in the query.
This is only done if we have enough schema information about the tables in the query.

The issue reported in #11880 is related to a derived table where we don't have enough information to expand the columns.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with vitess installed or the issue environment reproducible.
